criteria.description: A new pipecat-free module `sessions.py` exists at repo root exposing a callable `list_study_sessions()` and a module-level constant `COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` defaulting to the expanduser-resolved `~/second-brain/products/voice-tutor/validation/cost-log.jsonl`. Importing `sessions` must NOT cause `pipecat`, `bot`, `app`, or `fastapi` to be imported. Importing `documents` (and its transitive deps `anthropic`/`pypdf`) IS expected and allowed, since the helper resolves titles via `documents.load_document`.
criteria.verifyBy: A hermetic pytest imports `sessions` and asserts `callable(sessions.list_study_sessions)` and `hasattr(sessions, 'COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH')`. It also asserts that after importing `sessions`, none of `'pipecat'`, `'bot'`, `'app'`, `'fastapi'` are present in `sys.modules` as a result of that import (checked in the Pipecat-free verifier venv / conftest stub environment). It explicitly does NOT forbid `documents`/`anthropic`/`pypdf`. `python -c 'import sessions'` succeeds Pipecat-free.

criteria.description: `list_study_sessions()` returns study sessions NEWEST FIRST, ordered by `session_start` descending using ISO-8601 lexical (string) comparison. Each row is a mapping with exactly the five keys `session_id`, `document_title`, `session_start`, `session_duration_sec`, `cost_total_usd`. All carried-through values (`session_start` ISO string, `session_duration_sec` number, `cost_total_usd` number, `session_id`) are passed through UNMODIFIED — the helper performs no reformatting (no `"$1.39"` cost strings, no date humanization; formatting is the Sprint 2 UI's job). Ordering among rows with EQUAL `session_start` is not required to be strict; ties may hold any stable/arbitrary relative order and MUST NOT raise.
criteria.verifyBy: Hermetic pytest monkeypatches `sessions.COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` to a seeded `tmp_path` ledger containing at least 3 study rows where the ledger append order differs from the `session_start`-descending order (so append-order and sort-order are distinguishable). Asserts: (a) result order is newest-first by `session_start` descending; (b) each row's key set equals exactly {`session_id`,`document_title`,`session_start`,`session_duration_sec`,`cost_total_usd`}; (c) numeric fields remain numbers and `session_start` remains the raw ISO string (no reformatting). A separate case seeds two rows with an identical `session_start` and asserts both are present with no exception raised (tie handling).

criteria.description: Filtering: a row qualifies iff `kind == 'session'`, `mode == 'study'`, and `document_id` is non-null (present and not None). Excluded: open-chat rows (mode != 'study' or missing mode), doc-less rows (null/missing `document_id`), non-session rows (`kind != 'session'`), and specifically `kind == 'artifact'` rows. NOTE — the doc-less exclusion INTENTIONALLY DIVERGES from the `/api/sessions/latest` reference join, which does not apply that exclusion: a `mode == 'study'` row with null/missing `document_id` is EXCLUDED ENTIRELY here (it is NOT returned with `document_title=None`; that non-null-but-unresolvable case is c5's concern). The filter must qualify a row BEFORE extracting the five output fields, so rows that are mis-tagged or lack `session_start`/`session_duration_sec`/`cost_total_usd` never raise `KeyError` (use `.get()`-style access or filter-before-extract).
criteria.verifyBy: Hermetic pytest seeds a ledger mixing: (a) a valid study row; (b) an open-chat row (mode != 'study' / missing mode); (c) a doc-less study row (null/missing `document_id`); (d) a generic non-session row (`kind != 'session'`); and (e) — the real ledger confounder from `bot.py` — a `kind == 'artifact'` row that carries a valid non-null `document_id` (and shares a `session_id` with the valid study row) but LACKS `session_start`/`session_duration_sec`/`cost_total_usd`. Asserts: only the valid study row appears; the doc-less study row is absent (not present with `document_title=None`); the artifact row is absent; and NO exception (e.g. KeyError) is raised despite missing output fields on non-qualifying rows.

criteria.description: `document_title` is resolved exactly as the reference `/api/sessions/latest` join in app.py: `loaded = documents.load_document(document_id)` returns a tuple `(title, text)` or `None`, and `document_title = loaded[0] if loaded else None`. No non-existent `.title` attribute access is used.
criteria.verifyBy: Hermetic pytest seeds a document via the existing `docs_dir` fixture / documents store and a matching study ledger row; asserts the returned `document_title` equals the seeded document's title (i.e. `documents.load_document(document_id)[0]`). Test does not import `app`.

criteria.description: A qualifying study row whose `document_id` is non-null but does NOT resolve (e.g. document was deleted, so `documents.load_document` returns `None`) is still returned, with `document_title == None`. It is neither dropped from the list nor does it raise an error (mirroring the reference implementation's `loaded[0] if loaded else None`). This is distinct from c3's doc-less (null `document_id`) exclusion.
criteria.verifyBy: Hermetic pytest seeds a study ledger row with a non-null `document_id` that has no corresponding document in the `docs_dir` store; asserts the row is present in the result with `document_title is None` and no exception is raised.

criteria.description: Defensive parsing: malformed / non-JSON lines in the ledger are skipped via try/except around `json.loads` and are never fatal; a valid study row appearing after a malformed line is still returned.
criteria.verifyBy: Hermetic pytest seeds a ledger with a garbage/non-JSON line interleaved before a valid study row; asserts no exception is raised and the valid row is returned.

criteria.description: Empty ledger and absent (non-existent path) ledger both yield an empty list without error.
criteria.verifyBy: Two hermetic pytest cases: (a) `sessions.COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` points to an empty file under `tmp_path` → `list_study_sessions() == []`; (b) `sessions.COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` points to a non-existent path under `tmp_path` → `list_study_sessions() == []`.

criteria.description: The helper reads `COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` from the `sessions` module namespace at CALL time (not bound into a local at import time), so `monkeypatch.setattr(sessions, 'COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH', <tmp_path>)` takes effect for subsequent calls — mirroring the call-time attribute-read subtlety conftest relies on for `grounding.WIKI_DIR` / `documents.DOCUMENTS_DIR`.
criteria.verifyBy: Hermetic pytest imports `sessions` once, then within a single test monkeypatches `sessions.COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` to a seeded tmp ledger and asserts the call reads from the patched path (returns the seeded rows), and separately patches it to a non-existent path and asserts `[]` — proving the path is resolved at call time, not import time.

criteria.description: A snapshot guard proves the real vault cost-log at the default `COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` is never mutated by the session tests, mirroring the existing conftest `_snapshot` sha256-content pattern. The guard must handle the real file being ABSENT (represent 'file absent' as a distinct captured state; do not force-create it).
criteria.verifyBy: A pytest fixture captures the state of the real vault cost-log before and after the session tests — either its sha256-of-bytes (if present) or an 'absent' sentinel (if not present) — and asserts the state is unchanged. mtime is NOT used. All session tests operate against `tmp_path` via monkeypatch of `sessions.COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH`.

criteria.description: A thin FastAPI route `GET /api/sessions` is added in `app.py` whose body performs NO parsing, filtering, sorting, `json.loads`, or file `.open()` — it delegates entirely to `sessions.list_study_sessions()` and returns its result (an `async def` returning/awaiting the helper call is acceptable).
criteria.verifyBy: Static inspection of the `app.py` diff: the new `GET /api/sessions` handler contains only a call to `sessions.list_study_sessions()` and a return of that value, with no inline parsing/filtering/sorting/json/file logic. NOT verified by importing `app` or constructing a `TestClient`.

criteria.description: No regressions and no forbidden edits: `bot.py`, the voice pipeline, pipecat imports, the `/chat/` prebuilt-UI routes (`POST /start`, `POST /sessions/{id}/api/offer`, `PATCH /sessions/{id}/api/ice-candidate`), and the existing `/api/sessions/latest` route remain intact; no files deleted.
criteria.verifyBy: `pytest` run from repo root is green with the full pre-existing suite passing (unweakened). Static diff review confirms `bot.py`/pipeline/`/chat/` routes/`/api/sessions/latest` are untouched and no files removed.

criteria.description: The new hermetic session tests live in `tests/` and MUST NOT import `app.py`, import `pipecat`, or construct a `TestClient`; they follow the existing conftest fixture conventions (monkeypatching `sessions.COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH` to `tmp_path`).
criteria.verifyBy: Static inspection of the new test file confirms absence of `import app`, `from app import`, `import pipecat`, and `TestClient`; `pytest` collects and passes these tests Pipecat-free.

criteria.description: A new `#screen-history` section is added to static/study.html as a `.screen` sibling following the existing screen pattern (same class/structure as sibling screens such as `#screen-session` / `#screen-ended`). Back navigation from the history screen returns to the picker using the file's EXISTING shared `#header-back` control and lands on `showState('picker')`. The generator must reuse the existing shared header back mechanism — it must NOT introduce a new or duplicate back control for the history screen, and must NOT break the existing one.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection of static/study.html: confirm a new element with id `screen-history` and class `screen` exists, structured like sibling `.screen` sections; confirm that returning from `#screen-history` to the picker relies on the EXISTING shared `#header-back` control (whose handler calls `showState('picker')`), not a newly-added or duplicated back control; confirm this reuse does not redefine, duplicate, or break the existing back handler.

criteria.description: Because the shared back button `.wordmark__back` is `display:none` by default and only shown for a `data-context` allowlist (the file gates back-affordance visibility per context), `#screen-history`/the `history` context is integrated into that SAME mechanism so the back affordance is actually visible/usable on the history screen (the user is not trapped with no visible way back). The generator must mirror the existing allowlist/context pattern rather than invent a new visibility scheme.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: confirm that the history context is added to the SAME per-context visibility mechanism the file uses to reveal the shared back control on other non-picker screens (e.g. the CSS `data-context` allowlist / comma-separated selector group near lines 110–113, or the equivalent mechanism actually present), so the back affordance renders on the history screen; confirm pre-existing context entries in that allowlist are preserved unchanged.

criteria.description: The `screenFor` map inside `showState(...)` gains a `'history': 'screen-history'` entry (so showState('history') reveals `#screen-history` without throwing at `$(screenFor[name])`), and all pre-existing `screenFor` entries remain present and unchanged. Entering the history state also sets whatever per-screen state (e.g. the `data-context` value set via `wordmark.setAttribute('data-context', name)`, or an active-screen class) the file's OTHER screens set on entry, using the same mechanism — so the context-gated back affordance (per c2) and styling behave consistently.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: locate the `screenFor` object literal inside `showState` and confirm (a) a new `'history': 'screen-history'` key/value entry is added, (b) every previously existing key/value pair in that map is still present (none removed, renamed, or repointed), and (c) entering the history screen sets the same per-screen state (context/class/etc.) that existing screens set on entry, via the same mechanism the file already uses.

criteria.description: On entering the history screen the code performs `fetch('/api/sessions')` and renders the returned list preserving the response order (newest-first as delivered by the endpoint); the render must NOT reverse or re-sort into ascending/oldest-first order.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: confirm a fetch call to '/api/sessions' exists in the history-entry code path and its JSON array is iterated in natural response order to build DOM rows; confirm there is no `.reverse()`, no ascending/descending sort, and no other reordering applied to the response before rendering.

criteria.description: Each rendered history row displays all four fields co-located within that single row element: (a) document title from `document_title`; (b) a human-readable calendar date derived from `session_start` (NOT the raw ISO string emitted verbatim, and NOT a raw numeric epoch/timestamp integer — it must render as human-readable calendar date text); (c) a duration derived from `session_duration_sec` converted from seconds to a compact unit such as minutes (e.g. "8m"), NOT raw seconds; (d) cost derived from `cost_total_usd` formatted with a leading `$` and fixed 2 decimals (e.g. "$1.39"), NOT the raw float.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection of the row-render code: confirm each row element references and displays document_title; session_start passed through a genuine ISO-to-human date-formatting step producing human-readable calendar text (any real transformation qualifies — e.g. `new Date(...)` plus toLocaleDateString/toLocaleString OR a manual date-part formatter — named methods are illustrative, not an exhaustive whitelist; failures are: emitting the raw ISO field verbatim, or emitting a raw numeric timestamp like a bare epoch integer); session_duration_sec converted to minutes/compact unit (e.g. division by 60 with an 'm' suffix); and cost_total_usd formatted with a leading '$' and two decimals (e.g. toFixed(2)). Confirm all four appear together inside each per-row element (not a titles-only degenerate render).

criteria.description: Empty-state and error handling are distinct: (a) when `/api/sessions` returns an empty array, the history screen renders the EXACT text "No past sessions yet." (this is DoD #4 and is non-negotiable — the exact string, on the empty-array path); (b) SEPARATELY, a failed or non-OK fetch (missing endpoint / non-2xx / JSON parse error) must NOT throw an uncaught error that leaves the screen blank — it must land on a graceful visible message. A path that only logs to console and leaves a blank screen does NOT satisfy (b); an error-path message does NOT substitute for the exact empty-array string in (a).
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: confirm (a) an empty-array branch (`length === 0` or equivalent) renders the exact literal string "No past sessions yet." — verify character-for-character; and (b) the fetch code path guards non-OK/error cases separately (e.g. `response.ok` check and/or `.catch`) so an empty/failed fetch does not throw uncaught and leaves a visible graceful message rather than a blank screen. Confirm the exact empty-array string in (a) is not diluted or replaced by the generic error message in (b).

criteria.description: Clicking a history row invokes the EXISTING `loadSessionView(sid)` function, passing that row's `session_id` value (not an array index and not `document_id`); the `session_id` passed must originate from the SAME per-row datum whose four fields are rendered (guard against loop-variable/closure capture bugs — e.g. a `var`-scoped loop index or shared mutable reference capturing a stale value). Recap/telemetry/diagnostics rendering is NOT reimplemented in the history code path.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: confirm each row's click handler calls loadSessionView(...) with the row's session_id field specifically (not index, not document_id); confirm the session_id bound to the click handler comes from the same per-row object whose fields are rendered and is not subject to a loop-variable/closure capture bug (e.g. uses `let`/`const` per-iteration binding, a forEach/map callback parameter, a dataset attribute read at click time, or an equivalent per-row capture — not a shared `var` mutated across iterations); confirm no new recap/diagnostics/telemetry rendering logic is introduced in the history code path.

criteria.description: A prominent "Past sessions →" entry is added inside `#screen-picker`, DOM-ordered before the `Documents` section-label and the document list, clicking it opens the history screen via showState('history'). The entry must be a rendered, visible top-of-picker control — NOT hidden via `display:none`, `hidden`, `visibility:hidden`, or equivalent.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: confirm within `#screen-picker`, positioned in DOM order before the Documents section-label/document-list markup, a new labeled control whose visible text contains "Past sessions" with an arrow affordance (e.g. "Past sessions →") exists; confirm its handler calls showState('history') (not loadSessionView and not any other screen); and confirm it is not rendered hidden (no `display:none`/`hidden`/`visibility:hidden`/equivalent applied to it) so it functions as a prominent top-of-picker primary control.

criteria.description: The diff is confined strictly to `static/study.html`. No Python files are edited — in particular NO `app.py`, `sessions.py`, test, or other Python changes. `GET /api/sessions` is a Sprint 1 precondition assumed to already exist and return study sessions newest-first with fields session_id, document_title, session_start, session_duration_sec, cost_total_usd; this UI sprint does NOT add or modify that route. The pre-existing bottom `.last-session-row` / `#view-last-session` ("View last session →") markup and its click handler remain intact and functional, existing `.screen` sections are preserved (none renamed/deleted), and no per-user identity, cookies, client_id, ownership, search, filtering, pagination, regenerate-recap, or `/study/history` server route is introduced.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: confirm the diff touches ONLY static/study.html (no app.py, no sessions.py, no tests, no other Python or non-HTML files); confirm `.last-session-row`/`#view-last-session` markup and handler still exist unchanged; confirm existing `.screen` sections and their ids are preserved; confirm no added identity/cookie/client_id/ownership/search/filter/pagination/regenerate/`/study/history` constructs.

criteria.description: Existing screens and their showState behavior are not regressed: the shared `#header-back` handler, the `.last-session-row`/`#view-last-session` handler, and all pre-existing `screenFor`-driven screens continue to function as before, and the picker/session/ended/other screens still render. Additionally, the shared `#header-back` handler's routing (which calls `showState('picker')`) must NOT be modified to special-case the history screen — after a history-row click the user lands on the recap view (via `loadSessionView` → `showState('recap-ready')`) and back-to-picker is the intended, unchanged behavior.
criteria.verifyBy: Static diff inspection: confirm the added history markup/handlers/CSS do not clobber, rename, or repoint existing element ids, event handlers, `screenFor` entries, or existing context/CSS rules; confirm the shared `#header-back` handler still unconditionally routes to `showState('picker')` and was not altered to add history-specific branching; confirm the `#view-last-session` handler and existing screen sections remain wired exactly as before the change.

criteria.description: The existing pytest suite remains green and is not weakened by this sprint, and this sprint introduces zero Python and zero test-file changes (consistent with c9's HTML-only confinement). No pre-existing test may be deleted, skipped, or modified to make the suite pass.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `pytest` from repo root and confirm it passes; confirm no test files were modified, deleted, or skipped, and no Python files were changed at all (diff confined to static/study.html per c9).
